items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has launched the 2025-26 round of its Survey on International Trade in Banking Services (ITBS) . The survey, conducted annually since 2006-07, provides information on financial services rendered by branches/subsidiaries/joint ventures of Indian banks operating abroad and branches/subsidiaries of foreign banks operating in India, based on explicit / implicit fees / commission charged to customers. items.title: Shetland backs plan to connect islands with £1.5bn undersea tunnels
items.summary: The undersea links between some of the UK's most northerly islands could be in place within eight years.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank to acquire Deutsche Bank’s retail banking, private banking and wealth management business in India
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank is acquiring Deutsche Bank's retail, affluent private banking, and wealth management operations in India. This strategic move, involving approximately Rs 29,000 crore in loans and Rs 16,000 crore in deposits, aims to bolster Kotak's focus on affluent and SME segments. Deutsche Bank is divesting to simplify its business and concentrate on global ultra-high-net-worth clients.

items.title: Two general insurance licences approved since 100% FDI move by govt: Irdai chairman Ajay Seth
items.summary: India's insurance regulator has greenlit two proposals for 100% foreign ownership in general insurance firms, following the government's FDI liberalization. The much-anticipated BIMA Sugam platform, aiming for zero-commission policies, is slated for launch by September's end. Additionally, the Irdai is preparing distribution reforms to combat misselling and has established a Rs 50 crore fund for insurance awareness initiatives.

items.title: Govt extends customs duty waiver on import of key petrochemical products till July 15
items.summary: The government has extended the nil customs duty on approximately 40 critical petrochemical products by another 15 days, now until July 15. This move aims to maintain supply stability for sectors like plastics, packaging, and pharmaceuticals, which rely on these imports. The temporary relief, initially granted due to West Asia crisis-induced supply chain disruptions, seeks to ease cost pressures and ensure continued availability of essential industrial inputs.

items.title: India's fiscal deficit at 9.6% of FY27 target in May
items.summary: The government has budgeted a fiscal deficit of 4.3% of GDP, or about ₹16.96 trillion, for FY27 and is expected to rely on stronger tax collections and non-tax receipts in the coming months to keep the deficit within the target.

items.title: AI-enabled cyberattacks biggest near-term threat to financial system: RBI
items.summary: AI-powered cyberattacks are India's top financial system risk, according to the RBI's latest report. Banks and NBFCs are most concerned about these sophisticated threats, though preparedness is uneven. Rising third-party tech dependence and geopolitical uncertainty also heighten vulnerabilities. While institutions are investing more in cybersecurity, employee awareness and forensic capabilities need strengthening to combat evolving threats.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank to acquire Deutsche Bank's India retail banking business
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank has significantly expanded its footprint by acquiring Deutsche Bank's retail, private banking, and wealth management operations in India. This substantial deal, one of the largest in recent years, includes approximately ₹29,000 crore in loans and ₹16,000 crore in deposits. Around 1.5 lakh clients and nearly 1,000 employees will now join Kotak Mahindra Bank, bolstering its presence in the Indian financial landscape.

items.title: Small-cap stocks enjoy their best first half in 35 years. Here's what's driving it
items.summary: The advance marks a sharp turnaround after years of underperformance versus large-cap peers.

items.title: RBI stress test flags rising NBFC stress, capital buffers to thin by FY27
items.summary: India's central bank warns of rising bad loans and shrinking capital for non-banking lenders. Stressed assets could hit 2.8% by March 2027, with capital buffers dipping to 20.8% under normal conditions. Worryingly, several NBFCs might breach minimum capital requirements, especially under economic stress, highlighting ongoing regulatory focus on the sector's resilience.

items.title: Dalmia Bharat sees easing cost pressures, stays the course on expansion
items.summary: Dalmia Bharat says West Asia tensions are easing, but the impact of the war will continue to be felt through April-September before moderating

items.title: White House AI crackdown opens door for Chinese model makers to close gap
items.summary: The Trump administration's crackdown on Anthropic's leading artificial intelligence models is looking like a gift to China.

items.title: Nifty IT hits over 3-year low, crashes 30% year-to-date. Is it the right time to buy IT stocks?
items.summary: With the Nifty IT index hitting a 3-year low, analysts share insights on the evolving landscape of the sector. Despite cautious client spending and economic uncertainties, opportunities in AI-led services may present a compelling case for long-term investors. Is it the right moment to buy?

items.title: RBI flags AI stock boom as financial stability risk, says Indian banks remain resilient
items.summary: India's central bank has identified soaring AI stock valuations globally as a financial stability risk, warning of potential market spillovers. Despite this, the Reserve Bank of India asserts that India's banking system remains robust, well-capitalised, and resilient to external shocks. Strong macroeconomic fundamentals and healthy balance sheets in both banks and non-banking financial companies provide a solid foundation, limiting the impact of global financial stress on the domestic economy.

items.title: Rafael Nadal talks tennis prize money, his hotels, and what sports taught him about business
items.summary: Rafael Nadal opened his fourth Zel hotel as he expands into hospitality, education and sports after retiring from tennis.

items.title: Buffett delays annual donation to Gates Foundation pending review of Jeffrey Epstein ties: WSJ
items.summary: This comes as the foundation continues to review its ties to the late sex offender Jeffery Epstein.

items.title: Bad loans may edge up to 1.9% by FY28 despite resilient banks: RBI
items.summary: Indian banks are projected to see a slight increase in bad loans to 1.9% by March 2028, yet the system remains robust with strong capital and healthy profits. The Reserve Bank of India's report highlights resilient balance sheets and improving credit growth. Despite global uncertainties, the financial sector, including NBFCs, is well-positioned to absorb shocks, though AI cyber threats demand increased vigilance.

items.title: Manufacturing growth seen moderating in Q1 as West Asia crisis weighs on sentiment: FICCI
items.summary: India's manufacturing sector anticipates a slowdown in April-June FY27, influenced by West Asia tensions impacting business confidence. Despite this, robust domestic demand offers some resilience. Production and order book expectations have softened, though capacity utilization remains steady. Encouragingly, export performance shows improvement, driven by diversification efforts. However, cost pressures persist, and hiring plans have seen a slight dip.

items.title: Centre extends duty relief on key petrochemical products till July 15
items.summary: The government has extended the import duty exemption on about 40 key petrochemical products by 15 days, now until July 15. This move aims to support domestic industries facing supply chain disruptions and rising costs due to West Asian tensions. The temporary relief, initially introduced in April, helps sectors like plastics and pharmaceuticals by easing the impact on imported raw materials and freight costs.

items.title: Pension savings can help fund infrastructure, support Viksit Bharat: CEA
items.summary: India's burgeoning pension savings hold immense potential to fund infrastructure and propel the 'Viksit Bharat' vision, while ensuring stable returns for subscribers, according to Chief Economic Adviser V. Anantha Nageswaran. He emphasized that pension funds' long-term nature aligns perfectly with development projects. Nageswaran cautioned against excessive gold investments and the growing trend of short-term investing, stressing that financial security for the elderly is a key measure of a developed nation.

items.title: Oil prices set for steep monthly drop as Trump, Iran issue mixed messages on talks in Qatar
items.summary: The moves come as oil traders monitor the prospect of U.S.-Iran talks in Doha on Tuesday.

items.title: Medicare will start covering obesity drugs for the first time. Here's what patients should know
items.summary: The move could unlock millions of new patients for Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly and expand access to medications that were previously out of reach for seniors.

items.title: The $1 trillion AI 'arms race' is fueling a hidden supercycle and it's not just chips
items.summary: DBS Bank says the global AI arms race is driving a multi-year capex supercycle extending beyond semiconductors into energy, networking, infrastructure and data centres. The bank expects AI spending, sovereign AI investments and rising power demand to create new investment opportunities while reshaping portfolio allocation amid persistent inflation and geopolitical risks.

items.title: Flipkart bets on SuperCoins to outlast India's discount wars
items.summary: As e-commerce matures, loyalty programmes are replacing one-time discounts as the key driver of customer retention.

items.title: Abu Dhabi proposes new oil-pricing system to mirror wider market
items.summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. is proposing a shift in how it prices crude for term customers, aligning with regional trading norms. The company is considering setting official selling prices for Upper Zakum, Das, and Umm Lulu grades based on a differential to the Dubai benchmark, a move that could simplify comparisons with other regional crudes and support increased production following the UAE's exit from OPEC.

items.title: Gold heads for worst quarter in more than a decade as retail frenzy fades
items.summary: Expectations of higher interest rates fuelled by Iran war help end bullion’s record rally

items.title: MSC-Adani deal: Vizhinjam port can generate Rs 1,410 crore EBITDA, says Axis Capital
items.summary: Axis Capital expects Vizhinjam port to become a major earnings driver for Adani Ports & SEZ, estimating it could generate around Rs 1,410 crore in EBITDA after the Phase 2 expansion. The brokerage believes MSC's $1.4 billion investment strengthens volume visibility while reinforcing the port's long-term strategic and valuation potential.

items.title: GMR Airports among 7 stocks hit 52-week highs, rally up to 20% in a month
items.summary: Seven BSE 200 stocks, led by GMR Airports and FSN E-Commerce Ventures, scaled fresh 52-week highs despite a weak broader market, gaining up to 20% over the past month.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Stock markets decline for 2nd day as IT shares lead losses
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Benchmark stock indices Sensex and Nifty declined on Tuesday due to selling in IT, oil & gas and select banking shares amid uncertainty over the next round of US-Iran negotiations in Doha.

items.title: Rupee falls 15 paise to close at 94.65 against US dollar
items.summary: At the interbank foreign exchange market, the rupee opened at 94.60 against the American currency and finally closed for the day at 94.65 (provisional), registering a decline of 15 paise from its previous close

items.title: Battery metals futures heat up as volatility stirs markets
items.summary: Trading of cobalt and lithium futures contracts has risen this year on the leading European and US exchanges

items.title: India plans to cut reliance on West Asian oil after war shock
items.summary: The change to India’s oil-buying strategy would mark one of the biggest shifts in years, and will require balancing energy security needs while keeping crude-purchasing costs in check

items.title: India's April-May fiscal deficit at ₹1.62 lakh crore, widens on-year to 9.6% of FY27 aim
items.summary: India's fiscal deficit stood at ₹1.62 lakh crore during April-May, accounting for 9.6% of the full-year budget estimate, according to government data released on Tuesday.

items.title: Noel Tata steps down as Voltas chairman days after Trent exit
items.summary: Addressing shareholders of the leading cooling products maker in its 72nd annual general meeting (AGM), Noel Tata, 69, said this would be his last AGM as Chairman of Voltas.

items.title: Radhakishan Damani portfolio: These two dividend stocks yield consistent cash for investors
items.summary: Dividend-paying stocks attract long-term investors seeking income and capital appreciation. Radhakishan Damani's portfolio includes VST Industries and Advani Hotels, both with strong dividend histories.

items.title: Noel Tata announces exit as Voltas chairman at company's AGM
items.summary: Veteran Tata Group leader Noel Tata is stepping down as Chairman of Voltas, marking his final AGM in the role. He expressed confidence in the company's next-generation leadership, developed through internal promotions and external hires. Tata, who also recently announced his departure from Trent's chairmanship, cited the company's strong performance, including its air conditioning business exceeding one million unit sales in a record 81 days.

items.title: India plans to cut reliance on Middle East oil, tap spot market after war shock
items.summary: India's state refiners are pivoting away from Middle Eastern oil contracts following the Iran war's supply disruptions. They plan to boost spot market purchases and forge deals with trading houses for diversified sourcing. This strategic shift aims to secure energy supplies and mitigate losses, with new avenues opening from Guyana, Brazil, and the US. The nation is also building strategic reserves to enhance energy security.

items.title: India's youngest self-made billionaire is looking for a room on D-Street
items.summary: A teenager once pitched ₹30 lakh for a slice of his startup and mostly got no for an answer. That same idea now heads toward the public markets as a global hospitality business, built through coding experiments, budget stays, early failures, and a few investor regrets along the way. What started as a rough bet on fixing travel has turned into a system built on consistency. And as it moves toward listing, it feels less like a victory lap and more like a long “not convinced” finally arriving at its price.

items.title: Transformers and Rectifiers bags order worth Rs 1,000 cr from Power Grid Corporation of India
items.summary: Transformers and Rectifiers (India) Limited has secured a significant order exceeding Rs 1,000 crore from Power Grid Corporation of India Ltd (PGCIL). This "ultra mega order" involves the manufacturing of various rated transformers and all associated work. The substantial deal highlights a major development for the company in the power infrastructure sector.

items.title: Below-normal monsoon won't derail rural demand, says Nomura's Mihir Shah
items.summary: Despite fears of a weaker monsoon, Nomura's Mihir Shah anticipates muted impact on rural demand due to healthy reservoir levels and improved irrigation. El Niño's influence is expected to be limited until later in the season, with potential offsetting factors. Urban demand is showing signs of a pickup, narrowing the rural-urban gap. ITC faces near-term challenges from tax hikes, while Asian Paints remains a top pick.

items.title: Uber launches in-app ride video recording, ambulance feature in India; Nitin Gadkari lauds road safety push
items.summary: The new announcements were made during Uber Surakshit: Safety Never Stops , Uber India's flagship safety conference held at a Central Delhi hotel on Tuesday. Nitin Gadkari, Union Minister for Road Transport & Highways was the chief guest of the occasion.

items.title: Rupee ekes out first quarterly gain in five on oil drop, policy salvo
items.summary: The Indian rupee achieved its first quarterly gain since March 2025, buoyed by falling oil prices and policy efforts to attract dollar inflows. Despite a slight dip on Tuesday, the currency showed resilience, recovering from record lows. Analysts anticipate a stronger growth forecast for India, though importers' demand and central bank reserve rebuilding may limit further rupee appreciation.

items.title: India's Russian oil imports to hit record high in June, data shows
items.summary: India's Russian oil imports hit an all-time high in June, reaching approximately 2.70 million barrels per day. This surge is attributed to refiners seeking Russian barrels to offset potential supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz. Russian oil now constitutes over half of India's total crude imports, significantly increasing its dominance as the primary supplier.

items.title: India's chip dreams get a Rs 1.25 lakh crore push with Semiconductor Mission 2.0
items.summary: India is significantly boosting its semiconductor ambitions with a Rs 1.25 lakh crore allocation for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, a substantial increase from its predecessor. This move signals a deep commitment to establishing India as a global chip-making hub, focusing on equipment, materials, indigenous designs, and resilient supply chains.

items.title: Market wrap: Sensex falls 250 points, Nifty closes below 23,900 on monthly expiry day
items.summary: Indian equities ended lower for the second straight session as benchmarks slipped on expiry-day volatility, though broader markets advanced. IT stocks led declines amid rate worries, while auto and consumption names gained. Analysts expect sectoral rotation ahead of earnings, with banking, healthcare and power seen as resilient drivers of performance.

items.title: Stitching together growth: Modenik bets on legacy brands in India's innerwear battle
items.summary: The strategy comes as India's innerwear market, particularly women's lingerie, has become one of the country's most fiercely contested apparel categories, with established retailers and digital-first brands vying for market share.

items.title: NRI women investing nearly 70% higher in western markets than gulf countries: Report
items.summary: Female participation in cross-border investing among NRIs rose nearly 70% over the past year, according to Belong’s analysis of investors across 34 countries. Women accounted for 7.3% of NRI investors in June 2026, up from 4.3% a year earlier. The study also found sharp regional variations, with participation in Western markets nearly 1.7 times higher than in Gulf nations.

items.title: Rolex Rings sets July 3 as record date for ₹180-crore share buyback
items.summary: Rolex Rings will conduct a share buyback of up to 10 million equity shares at ₹180 each, with July 3, 2026, as the record date. The buyback follows a board approval and aims to stabilize stock prices amid recent declines.

items.title: Mag 7 value shrinks by $2.3 trillion amid AI spending jitters — but investors are still backing chipmakers
items.summary: Investors are growing jittery about massive AI spending by the Mag 7 and when these companies will produce a return on that investment.

items.title: Stock market today: Sensex, Nifty 50 extend losses for 2nd consecutive session; mid, small-caps outperform
items.summary: Stock market today: The Sensex ended 250 points, or 0.33%, lower at 76,478.67, while the Nifty 50 dropped 81 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 23,865.75. The Nifty Midcap 100 index climbed 0.37%, while the Smallcap 100 index jumped 1.02%.

items.title: Waaree Solar Americas gets 236 MW solar module supply order in US
items.summary: Waaree Solar Americas has landed a significant 236 MW module supply deal for a major solar project in Flemingsburg, Kentucky. These modules will be produced at their Texas facility, underscoring Waaree's commitment to bolstering America's clean energy infrastructure through domestic manufacturing. This contract further solidifies the US as a crucial market for the company's growth and clean energy initiatives.

items.title: Slumdogs no more: Can tycoons take the Mumbai Model to other cities?
items.summary: India's top business tycoons like Adani and Ambani are shifting focus from city outskirts to redeveloping Mumbai's older housing and slum areas. This strategic move, driven by scarce and expensive greenfield land, aims to transform decaying urban pockets. Major projects are underway, signaling a new era of large-scale urban regeneration that could serve as a model for cities nationwide.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz: Strategic chokepoint in shadow of geopolitics
items.summary: The Strait of Hormuz, a key chokepoint for the global trade of crude oil and liquefied natural gas, is at the centre of disputes in negotiations to end the Middle East war, even after the US and Iran signed a preliminary deal. About one-fifth of consumption of global oil and petroleum products flows through the strait, averaging 20 million barrels per day in 2024, according to the EIA.

items.title: Digital Realty falls 5% after taking $3.5 billion stake in Blackstone's Virginia data centers
items.summary: Digital Realty fell in premarket trading Tuesday after it announced its buying a $3.5 billion stake in three data centers from asset manager Blackstone.

items.title: Coal India to invest ₹1,900 crore in R&D by FY30
items.summary: Coal India Ltd is set to invest nearly ₹1,900 crore in R&D by FY2030 to boost mine productivity and cut emissions. The company is focusing on cleaner coal technologies and alternative energy sources, with its R&D spending significantly increasing this fiscal. New initiatives include a National Centre for Coal and Energy Research and collaborations with international partners for advanced projects.

items.title: Bitcoin remains stuck below $60,000 as traders eye Fed outlook and institutional demand
items.summary: Bitcoin traded below $60,000 as markets remained cautious amid expectations of U.S. monetary policy signals and mixed institutional flows. Price action stayed range-bound with limited momentum despite ETF inflows and ongoing macro uncertainty. Altcoins showed mixed performance while traders monitored Federal Reserve direction and broader liquidity conditions across global markets.

items.title: Jet Airways employees get PF, gratuity relief as NCLAT rejects SBI's plea against priority payout amid liquidation
items.summary: The NCLAT has dismissed SBI's appeal, confirming priority payment of PF and gratuity dues to Jet Airways employees during liquidation. Details here.

items.title: How Delta Exchange India makes crypto trading simple and affordable
items.summary: Crypto trading is booming in India's smaller cities, with Delta Exchange India emerging as a key player. The platform offers low fees, full INR settlement, and features catering to both beginners and advanced traders. Notably, F&O trading on Delta Exchange avoids the 1% TDS and 30% flat tax, making it an attractive option for active participants seeking affordability and regulatory compliance.

items.title: Yen tumbles to 4-decade low. Here are 3 reasons behind the historic slump
items.summary: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said authorities were ready to respond appropriately at any time, without giving stronger signals. The sharp decline has fuelled growing speculation that Tokyo could be edging closer to direct intervention to support the currency.

items.title: Ford says AI failed to match human expertise, rehires 350 engineers: 'Only as Good as its training'
items.summary: Charles Poon, Ford's vice president of vehicle hardware engineering, said the company underestimated the value of its most experienced workforce while expanding its use of AI.

items.title: French biotech stock soars 34% after new data on experimental medicine
items.summary: Shares of French biotech company Abivax soared Tuesday after it released fresh data on its lead drug for bowel disease.

items.title: Binance intercepts $10.5 billion in frauds using AI, invests $300 million per year in compliance
items.summary: Binance said its AI-powered compliance systems intercepted $10.53 billion in potential fraud between 2025 and the first quarter of 2026, while the crypto exchange invests around $300 million annually in compliance. The company said the spending supports fraud prevention, law-enforcement collaboration, user protection and asset recovery efforts.

items.title: Cement sector faces muted Q1 FY27 as heat, water scarcity hit demand: Ashika's Jyoti Gupta
items.summary: India's cement sector faces a subdued start to FY27, with extended heat and water scarcity dampening construction despite a delayed monsoon. Analysts anticipate a significant slowdown in demand growth, with volumes projected at 4-5% compared to last year's 7-8%. Margins are also under pressure from rising costs, with a recovery expected in the latter half of the fiscal year.

items.title: Indonesia jails former education minister for 10 years in Google Chromebook graft case
items.summary: Makarim was found guilty in a case over the procurement of Google Chromebooks for schools

items.title: WhatsApp to let people chat by swapping usernames instead of phone numbers
items.summary: The app's new usernames feature will be rolled out globally over the next few months.

items.title: Gold futures rise to ₹1.42 lakh/10g after early losses
items.summary: Gold futures recovered as traders reassessed the US interest rate outlook amid lingering geopolitical tensions and volatile crude oil prices

items.title: Silver rebounds to ₹2.26 lakh/kg after early drop as global prices recover
items.summary: Analysts said the recovery in the precious metal was in line with firm trends in the global markets

items.title: Penny stock under ₹10 hits 5% upper circuit. Details here
items.summary: A-1 Limited's shares reached ₹7.30, capped at a 5% limit, after becoming the primary dealer for Ishan Dyes' sulphur chemicals. This partnership enhances A-1's market presence and is expected to drive revenue growth in FY2026-27 and beyond.

items.title: Gold vs Silver: Which commodity offers better return potential in H2 CY26?
items.summary: During the first half of CY26, Comex gold prices have fallen more than 7%, while silver has corrected over 16%. In the domestic market, while MCX gold price has gained roughly 4% on a in 2026 so far, MCX silver price has declined more than 5% during the same period.

items.title: Upcoming IPO: Fibe parent Social Worth Technologies files DRHP with SEBI for IPO
items.summary: Parent company of Fibe, Social Worth Technologies Ltd, filed for an IPO aiming to raise up to ₹750 crore. Proceeds will support its subsidiary EarlySalary Services and corporate needs. Founded in 2015, it has diversified lending and improved profitability significantly since 2021.

items.title: Monsoon blues, weak demand dampen July outlook for cement prices
items.summary: Cement prices are expected to hold steady in July, with the monsoon and sluggish demand dampening prospects for significant hikes. While June saw a slight uptick due to delayed rains and quarter-end sales, dealers anticipate continued pricing pressure. Efforts to raise prices are likely aimed at defending current levels rather than substantial increases, as construction activity remains subdued.

items.title: Inflation likely to stay ‘significantly above target,’ top European central banker warns
items.summary: Joachim Nagel, president of Germany’s Bundesbank, spoke to CNBC on the sidelines of an ECB conference in Portugal.

items.title: Coal India to invest $201 million in research and development by fiscal 2030

items.summary: European markets are soaring, marking their best quarterly performance in over five years.
